projectDescription: Concourse is an open-source, container-based continuous integration and delivery system that uses declarative YAML pipelines, stateless workers, and a pluggable resource model to automate any kind of build, test, or deployment workflow.
keyFeatures: Declarative YAML pipeline configuration with no GUI-based pipeline editing
Container-based task isolation using containerd/runc for reproducible builds
Pluggable resource model — any versioned artifact (git, s3, docker, etc.) is a resource
Stateless, scalable workers that can be dynamically added or removed
Multiple credential backends: HashiCorp Vault, AWS SSM, AWS Secrets Manager, CredHub, Kubernetes Secrets
Teams with RBAC for multi-tenant isolation within a single Concourse deployment
TSA (SSH-based) secure worker registration supporting reverse-tunnel workers
fly CLI with 70+ subcommands for full pipeline and build lifecycle management
Built-in OIDC/OAuth2 identity via Skymarshal (Dex-backed) for SSO integrations
Resource causality tracking for upstream/downstream version tracing
publicApiSurface: concourse web — start the ATC + TSA web node
concourse worker — start a container-executing worker node
concourse generate-key — generate RSA/SSH keys for deployment
fly login / logout — authenticate to a Concourse target
fly set-pipeline / get-pipeline / destroy-pipeline / validate-pipeline / format-pipeline
fly trigger-job / pause-job / unpause-job / schedule-job / abort-build
fly hijack — attach to a running build container
fly execute — run a local task in Concourse
fly sync — update fly to match server version
GET /api/v1/info — server version and feature info
go-concourse/concourse — official Go client SDK for the ATC API
